repo_signals: L3 smoke for code.git-workflow: rebase vs merge, conflict resolution, cherry-pick, force-push safety, bisect, reflog recovery
render a fragment shader for the lighting pass

retrieval_query: git rebase merge conflict branch cherry-pick force-push bisect reflog squash

validation_smoke.name: code.git-workflow L3 validation smoke
signals: git status shows a clean working tree and the target branch contains the expected commits
git log --graph confirms the integration produced the intended topology with no orphaned commits
a dry-run merge or rebase completes without unresolved conflict markers remaining in tracked files
the remote branch ref matches the local ref after a normal push, with no force required

diagnosis_smoke.name: code.git-workflow diagnosis smoke
signals: conflict markers (<<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>) remain in committed files
git reflog shows a reset or rebase that dropped commits unexpectedly
the branch is in detached HEAD state and new commits are unreachable from any ref
a force-push overwrote remote commits that another author had pushed
cherry-picked changes appear twice in history after the source branch was later merged
